# Quality of life
- Use history command to get feedback and then decide what alias to make

# my_config refactor (2026-07): decisions and deferred items

Recorded during the numbered-chain refactor. Notes for future work, not open
bugs unless marked.


## Latent bugs found during the refactor

Resolved in supplementary patches:
- `11_git_utils.sh`: the "usb-repos" skip read the never-assigned `repo_dir`
  (should be `repo_name`) in four functions -- stash_report, pull_all_repos,
  push_all_repos, prune_merged_branches -- so the skip never fired. Fixed.
- `11_git_utils.sh` prune_merged: `current_branch` was computed but unused; it is
  now an explicit guard alongside the existing `* ` marker skip. Fixed.
- `04_verify.sh`: the symlink-mismatch worktree heuristic read the undefined
  `MC_REPO_ROOT` (should be `MC_ROOT`), so every mismatch was demoted to a
  worktree warning and never counted as an error. Fixed.
- `00_config.sh`: `MC_REPOS_ROOT` was exported but never defined; removed from
  the export list. (Making the repos root a real, wired setting -- the git
  helpers currently hardcode `$HOME/personal_repos` -- remains a possible
  enhancement.)
- `01_activate.sh`: `VISUAL` was set from the leaked editor-loop variable rather
  than the chosen `EDITOR`; now set from `$EDITOR`. Fixed.

Still open:
- `01_activate.sh`: lesspipe is initialized via `eval` (standard idiom, fixed
  command; intentionally left as-is).

## Deferred: MC_PS1 non-idempotency (external usb-sh)
On re-source (`mc_reload`) the USB prompt indicator is lost. `00_config.sh` sets
`MC_PS1` to a constant on each source, and `usb-sh/usb.sh` (sourced by
`06_usb.sh`) prepends the indicator under a one-time guard that does not
re-apply after the reset. The my_config side is idempotent; the fix belongs in
usb-sh (re-apply when the prefix is absent, or drive the indicator from
`PROMPT_COMMAND`).

## Decisions made during the refactor
- History variables: `01_activate.sh` now reads the canonical `MC_HIST_*` names.
  Previously it read `MC_HISTCONTROL`/`MC_HISTSIZE`/`MC_HISTFILESIZE`, which were
  undefined and exported empty (behavior change).
- `MC_COLOR_SUPPORT` was removed: dead repo-wide (no consumer).
- `_is_inside_git_repo` is duplicated in `10_vim_utils.sh` and `11_git_utils.sh`
  rather than shared, to avoid a cross-file load-order dependency. No shared
  abstraction was adopted yet.
- Two toolchain scripts (`miniforge_setup.sh`, `rsync_git_repositories.sh`) are
  excluded from the lint shellcheck pass via a named skip-list (conda-managed /
  flagged for review). Alternative: in-place shebang + `[[ && ]]` fixes.
- The message engine was extracted to `lib/message.sh` (VERSION 1), with
  `03_message.sh` reduced to a thin shim.
- Settings use a `##` documentation convention parsed by
  `lib/config_inspect.awk` and surfaced by `mc_config`. This requires bash 4.3+
  (namerefs). The command/parser names were reconstructed during the refactor.
- `setup_symlinks.sh` was retired in favor of `bootstrap.sh`, which creates the
  symlinks declared in `MC_SYMLINKS`; the `vimrc.vim` link was migrated into
  `MC_SYMLINKS`.
- The double-source idempotency check ignores volatile terminal/timer variables
  (COLUMNS, LINES, EPOCHSECONDS, ...) so only genuine drift is reported.

## Possible follow-ups
- Colors: consolidated into `lib/message.sh` (removed from `00_config.sh`). If
  colors are later needed independently of the message engine, consider a
  standalone `lib/colors.sh` that both can source.
- Reconsider whether `vimrc.vim` (`~/.vimrc`) is still wanted now that nvim is
  primary.
